rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 23, 2024 line 1. DEFENDANT MALINKA MOYE WADE, AN INDIVIDUAL MOTION TO SET ASIDE FABR-$42,620.00 DEFAULT JUDGMENT. is DENIED. Defendant does not state grounds for setting aside default judgment, and none of the addresses in Defendant's Proofs of Service are the addresses of record for Plaintiff. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: KAITLYN JOHNSON ET AL VS. KATHLEEN MCHUGH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Confirm Arbitrtion Award; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 23, 2024 line 2. PETITIONER KAITLYN JOHNSON, CAROLINE GASPARINI NOTICE OF MOTION TO CONFIRM ARBITRTION AWARD; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT THEREOF is GRANTED. The award is confirmed as stated in the Final Award. Respondent first appeared in this action on June 27, 2024 stating that "this Court should confirm the Final Award as is," objecting only to additional language requested by the Petitioner. The proposed additional language shall not be included in the judgment and award is confirmed as is. In opposition to the Motion to Confirm filed on October 9, 2024 (long after the 100-day CCP 1288.2 deadline has passed) Respondent for the first time in this action claims that (1) no part of the award should be confirmed because Petitioner asked for additional language; no authority is cited for this proposition; (2) the award should be modified; no argument provided as to the requested modification; and (3) the award should be vacated per CCP 1286.2(a)(5) due to the Arbitrator's refusal to hear evidence material to the controversy. On the last point Respondent presents no evidence that Arbitrator "refused" to consider any evidence properly presented to him. Respondents also claim entitlement to equitable tolling and equitable estoppel re: 100-day deadline to file a request to vacate or correct the award. Respondents present no evidence to support each element of these equitable relief theories as it relates to Respondents' failure to seek to vacate/modify the award within 100 days. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
